Spokane aerie No. 2, Fraternal
Order of Eagles, last night elected
the following officers for the ensu
ing year: Worthy president, Judge
j. Stanley Webster; vice president
M. M. Richardson; chaplain, A. W.
Neimeyer; secretary, l.. j. Hender.
shot; treasurer, H. F. Sorg; inside
guard, H. E. Stage; outside guard
c. X. Thornton; trustees, H. N
Oerter, E. H. Shaw, Judge E. H.
Sullivan; physician, DR.. w. G.
Webb.


The EIks tonight will initiate a
class of IL members and the degree
work will be followed by banquet
and musical entertainment.


A canvass for a fund Of $4,000
to be used in the erection of a new
club building has been begun by
the Woman's club. Teh building
will be located convenient to the
business district, but the site has
not yet been selected. The club has
$4,000 in cash.
